block_00: Apple is heading South ...well, South West to be exact!
block_01: If you are an Apple consumer, chances are, you have no doubt seen the famous “Designed in California' but 'Assembled in China” stamp, at the back of their products.
block_02: By 2004, Apple had moved much of its production line to China, & has since continued to mass produce everything, from iPhones to iPads at its factory in Shenzhen.
block_03: The company responsible “Foxconn” has been running operations from there since. However it has been revealed that they are now “setting up shop” elsewhere; in India. It was reported that Foxconn’s labor costs are three times higher in China, than compared to India. In addition, the move hopes to aid in penetrating the Indian smartphone market, one which is already saturated in China.
block_04: Foxconn’s Chairman, Terry Gou, hopes that by producing phones locally, it will push Apple’s retail presence in India. Indians bought more than 140 million smartphones last year, with only 1.7 million sold by Apple.
block_05: With Apple currently 15% shy of reaching its previous all time high, it’s clear to see the smartphone giant has not given up its fight just yet.
block_06: ...an apple a day keeps the doctor away; but what about the trader?
